packet.c(3) Library Functions Manual packet.c(3)

NAME

packet.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t packet_send (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len)
Send a packet with proper header and CRC32. asciichat_error_t packet_receive (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t *type, void **data, size_t *len)
Receive a packet with proper header validation and CRC32 checking. asciichat_error_t send_packet_secure (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Send a packet with encryption and compression support. packet_recv_result_t receive_packet_secure (socket_t sockfd, void *crypto_ctx, bool enforce_encryption, packet_envelope_t *envelope)
Receive a packet with decryption and decompression support. int send_packet (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len)
Send a basic packet without encryption. int receive_packet (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t *type, void **data, size_t *len)
Receive a basic packet without encryption. int send_ping_packet (socket_t sockfd)
Send a ping packet. int send_pong_packet (socket_t sockfd)
Send a pong packet. asciichat_error_t packet_decrypt_envelope (packet_envelope_t *envelope, void *crypto_ctx)
Send a clear console packet. asciichat_error_t packet_send_error (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx, asciichat_error_t error_code, const char *message)
Send an error packet with optional encryption context. asciichat_error_t packet_parse_error_message (const void *data, size_t len, asciichat_error_t *out_error_code, char *message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t *out_message_length)
Parse an error packet payload into components. asciichat_error_t packet_send_remote_log (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx, log_level_t level, remote_log_direction_t direction, uint16_t flags, const char *message)
Send a remote log packet with optional encryption context. asciichat_error_t packet_parse_remote_log (const void *data, size_t len, log_level_t *out_level, remote_log_direction_t *out_direction, uint16_t *out_flags, char *message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t *out_message_length)
Parse a remote log packet payload into components. int send_protocol_version_packet (socket_t sockfd, const protocol_version_packet_t *version)
Send protocol version packet. int send_crypto_capabilities_packet (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_capabilities_packet_t *caps)
Send crypto capabilities packet. int send_crypto_parameters_packet (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_parameters_packet_t *params)
Send crypto parameters packet. asciichat_error_t av_send_audio_opus_batch (socket_t sockfd, const uint8_t *opus_data, size_t opus_size, const uint16_t *frame_sizes, int sample_rate, int frame_duration, int frame_count, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Send Opus-encoded audio batch packet with encryption support. asciichat_error_t send_image_frame_packet (socket_t sockfd, const void *image_data, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, uint8_t format)
Send image frame packet.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t packet_parse_error_message (const void * data, size_t len, asciichat_error_t * out_error_code, char * message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t * out_message_length)

Parse an error packet payload into components.

Parameters

data Packet payload buffer
len Payload length in bytes
out_error_code Output pointer for asciichat_error_t value (must not be NULL)
message_buffer Destination buffer for message string (must not be NULL)
message_buffer_size Size of destination buffer in bytes (must be > 0)
out_message_length Optional output for message length reported by sender

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Definition at line 939 of file packet.c.

941                                                                          {
942   if (!data || len < sizeof(error_packet_t) || !out_error_code || !message_buffer || message_buffer_size == 0) {
943     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
944                      "Invalid parameters: data=%p len=%zu out_error_code=%p message_buffer=%p buffer_size=%zu", data,
945                      len, out_error_code, message_buffer, message_buffer_size);
946   }
947 
948   const error_packet_t *packet = (const error_packet_t *)data;
949   uint32_t raw_error_code = NET_TO_HOST_U32(packet->error_code);
950   uint32_t raw_message_length = NET_TO_HOST_U32(packet->message_length);
951 
952   if (raw_message_length > MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH) {
953     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Error message length too large: %u", raw_message_length);
954   }
955 
956   size_t total_required = sizeof(error_packet_t) + (size_t)raw_message_length;
957   if (total_required > len) {
958     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Error packet truncated: expected %zu bytes, have %zu", total_required,
959                      len);
960   }
961 
962   const uint8_t *message_bytes = (const uint8_t *)data + sizeof(error_packet_t);
963   size_t copy_len = raw_message_length;
964   if (copy_len >= message_buffer_size) {
965     copy_len = message_buffer_size - 1;
966   }
967 
968   if (copy_len > 0) {
969     memcpy(message_buffer, message_bytes, copy_len);
970   }
971   message_buffer[copy_len] = '\0';
972 
973   if (out_message_length) {
974     *out_message_length = raw_message_length;
975   }
976 
977   *out_error_code = (asciichat_error_t)raw_error_code;
978   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
979 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, error_packet_t::error_code,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
    MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH, error_packet_t::message_length,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t packet_parse_remote_log (const void *
  data, size_t len, log_level_t * out_level,
  remote_log_direction_t * out_direction, uint16_t * out_flags,
  char * message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t *
  out_message_length)


Parse a remote log packet payload into components.


Parameters


data Packet payload buffer


len Payload length in bytes


out_level Output pointer for log level (must not be NULL)


out_direction Output pointer for direction (must not be NULL)


out_flags Output pointer for flags (must not be NULL)


message_buffer Destination buffer for message string (must not be NULL)


message_buffer_size Size of destination buffer in bytes (must be > 0)


out_message_length Optional output for message length reported by
  sender


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1038 of file packet.c.


1041                                                                       {
1042   if (!data || len < sizeof(remote_log_packet_t) || !out_level || !out_direction || !out_flags || !message_buffer ||
1043       message_buffer_size == 0) {
1044     return SET_ERRNO(
1045         ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1046         "Invalid parameters: data=%p len=%zu out_level=%p out_direction=%p out_flags=%p buffer=%p size=%zu", data, len,
1047         out_level, out_direction, out_flags, message_buffer, message_buffer_size);
1048   }
1049 
1050   const remote_log_packet_t *packet = (const remote_log_packet_t *)data;
1051   uint8_t raw_level = packet->log_level;
1052   if (raw_level > LOG_FATAL) {
1053     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid remote log level: %u", raw_level);
1054   }
1055   *out_level = (log_level_t)raw_level;
1056 
1057   uint8_t raw_direction = packet->direction;
1058   if (raw_direction > REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER) {
1059     raw_direction = REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN;
1060   }
1061   *out_direction = (remote_log_direction_t)raw_direction;
1062 
1063   uint16_t raw_flags = NET_TO_HOST_U16(packet->flags);
1064   *out_flags = raw_flags;
1065 
1066   uint32_t raw_message_length = NET_TO_HOST_U32(packet->message_length);
1067   if (raw_message_length > MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH) {
1068     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Remote log message length too large: %u", raw_message_length);
1069   }
1070 
1071   size_t total_required = sizeof(remote_log_packet_t) + (size_t)raw_message_length;
1072   if (total_required > len) {
1073     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Remote log packet truncated: expected %zu bytes, have %zu",
1074                      total_required, len);
1075   }
1076 
1077   const uint8_t *message_bytes = (const uint8_t *)data + sizeof(remote_log_packet_t);
1078   size_t copy_len = raw_message_length;
1079   if (copy_len >= message_buffer_size) {
1080     copy_len = message_buffer_size - 1;
1081   }
1082 
1083   if (copy_len > 0) {
1084     memcpy(message_buffer, message_bytes, copy_len);
1085   }
1086   message_buffer[copy_len] = '\0';
1087 
1088   if (out_message_length) {
1089     *out_message_length = raw_message_length;
1090   }
1091 
1092   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1093 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    remote_log_packet_t::direction, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, remote_log_packet_t::flags,
    LOG_FATAL, remote_log_packet_t::log_level,
    MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH,
    remote_log_packet_t::message_length, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER,
    REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by handle_remote_log_packet_from_client().






asciichat_error_t packet_send_error (socket_t
  sockfd, const crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx, asciichat_error_t
  error_code, const char * message)


Send an error packet with optional encryption context.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


crypto_ctx Crypto context for encryption (NULL or not ready sends
  plaintext)


error_code Error code from
  asciichat_error_t enumeration


message Human-readable message to accompany the error (can be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


When the crypto context is ready, the packet is encrypted
    automatically. During the handshake (or when encryption is disabled), the
    packet is sent in plaintext so protocol errors can be delivered before
    encryption is active.


Definition at line 892 of file packet.c.


893                                                          {
894   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
895     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket descriptor");
896   }
897 
898   if (!message) {
899     message = "";
900   }
901 
902   size_t message_len = strnlen(message, MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH);
903   if (message_len == MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH) {
904     log_warn("Error message truncated to %zu bytes", (size_t)MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH);
905   }
906 
907   size_t payload_len = sizeof(error_packet_t) + message_len;
908   uint8_t *payload = SAFE_MALLOC(payload_len, uint8_t *);
909   if (!payload) {
910     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for error packet", payload_len);
911   }
912 
913   error_packet_t *packet = (error_packet_t *)payload;
914   packet->error_code = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)error_code);
915   packet->message_length = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)message_len);
916 
917   if (message_len > 0) {
918     memcpy(payload + sizeof(error_packet_t), message, message_len);
919   }
920 
921   bool encryption_ready = crypto_ctx && crypto_is_ready(crypto_ctx);
922   asciichat_error_t send_result;
923 
924   if (encryption_ready) {
925     send_result =
926         send_packet_secure(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_ERROR_MESSAGE, payload, payload_len, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
927   } else {
928     send_result = packet_send(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_ERROR_MESSAGE, payload, payload_len);
929   }
930   SAFE_FREE(payload);
931 
932   if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
933     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send error packet: %s", asciichat_error_string(send_result));
934   }
935 
936   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
937 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_is_ready(),
    error_code, error_packet_t::error_code,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    HOST_TO_NET_U32, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_warn,
    MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH, error_packet_t::message_length,
    packet_send(), PACKET_TYPE_ERROR_MESSAGE, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, send_packet_secure(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by disconnect_client_for_bad_data().






asciichat_error_t packet_send_remote_log (socket_t
  sockfd, const crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx, log_level_t level,
  remote_log_direction_t direction, uint16_t flags, const char *
  message)


Send a remote log packet with optional encryption context.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


crypto_ctx Crypto context for encryption (NULL or not ready sends
  plaintext)


level Log level to transmit


direction Direction flag (REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_*)


flags Additional flags (REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_*)


message Log message text


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 981 of file packet.c.


982                                                                                                                 {
983   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
984     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket descriptor");
985   }
986 
987   if (level < LOG_DEV || level > LOG_FATAL) {
988     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid log level: %d", level);
989   }
990 
991   const char *safe_message = message ? message : "";
992   bool truncated = false;
993   size_t message_len = strnlen(safe_message, MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH);
994   if (message_len == MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH && safe_message[message_len] != '\0') {
995     truncated = true;
996   }
997 
998   size_t payload_len = sizeof(remote_log_packet_t) + message_len;
999   uint8_t *payload = SAFE_MALLOC(payload_len, uint8_t *);
1000   if (!payload) {
1001     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for remote log packet", payload_len);
1002   }
1003 
1004   remote_log_packet_t *packet = (remote_log_packet_t *)payload;
1005   packet->log_level = (uint8_t)level;
1006   packet->direction = (uint8_t)direction;
1007   uint16_t final_flags = flags;
1008   if (truncated) {
1009     final_flags |= REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_TRUNCATED;
1010   }
1011   packet->flags = HOST_TO_NET_U16(final_flags);
1012   packet->message_length = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)message_len);
1013 
1014   if (message_len > 0) {
1015     memcpy(payload + sizeof(remote_log_packet_t), safe_message, message_len);
1016   }
1017 
1018   bool encryption_ready = crypto_ctx && crypto_is_ready(crypto_ctx);
1019   asciichat_error_t send_result;
1020 
1021   if (encryption_ready) {
1022     int secure_result =
1023         send_packet_secure(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_REMOTE_LOG, payload, payload_len, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
1024     send_result = secure_result == 0 ? ASCIICHAT_OK : ERROR_NETWORK;
1025   } else {
1026     send_result = packet_send(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_REMOTE_LOG, payload, payload_len);
1027   }
1028 
1029   SAFE_FREE(payload);
1030 
1031   if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1032     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send remote log packet: %d", send_result);
1033   }
1034 
1035   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1036 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_is_ready(),
    remote_log_packet_t::direction, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    remote_log_packet_t::flags, HOST_TO_NET_U16,
    HOST_TO_NET_U32, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, LOG_FATAL,
    remote_log_packet_t::log_level, MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH,
    remote_log_packet_t::message_length, packet_send(),
    PACKET_TYPE_REMOTE_LOG, REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_TRUNCATED,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, send_packet_secure(), and
    SET_ERRNO.
